item.description: The U.S. launched what it called “self-defense strikes” after an American helicopter was shot down by an Iranian drone over the Strait of Hormuz, as President Trump vowed Iran would “pay the price.” NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

media:content.media:title: U.S. launches ‘self defense’ strikes on Iran after Apache helicopter shot down
media:content.media:description: The U.S. launched what it called “self-defense strikes” after an American helicopter was shot down by an Iranian drone over the Strait of Hormuz, as President Trump vowed Iran would “pay the price.” NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

item.title: U.S. launches ‘self defense’ strikes on Iran after Apache helicopter shot down

item.description: NBC News Correspondents Monica Alba, Courtney Kube and Brian Cheung join Meet the Press NOW to report on recent developments in the war with Iran after the President Trump vowed to attack the country “hard” again while inflation jumps to its highest point since 2023.

media:content.media:title: Trump says ‘I love the inflation’ as Iran war continues to spike prices
media:content.media:description: NBC News Correspondents Monica Alba, Courtney Kube and Brian Cheung join Meet the Press NOW to report on recent developments in the war with Iran after the President Trump vowed to attack the country “hard” again while inflation jumps to its highest point since 2023.

item.title: Trump says ‘I love the inflation’ as Iran war continues to spike prices

item.dc:creator: Gretchen Morgenson
item.description: In recent years, mobile home properties have become less affordable as an influx of large companies have bought up the parks and raised rents and fees.

media:content.media:text: Aliea Brown and Mason Obradovich at their home in Robinsonville, Miss.
media:content.media:description: Aliea Brown and Mason Obradovich at their home in Robinsonville, Miss.

media:content.media:text: Black mold and water damage have been found throughout Aliea Brown and Mason Obradovich's home.
media:content.media:description: Aliea Brown and Mason Obradovich at their home in Robinsonville, Miss.

media:content.media:text: Buck Island offered to sell the dilapidated unit to Brown for $1,000.
media:content.media:description: Aliea Brown and Mason Obradovich at their home in Robinsonville, Miss.

media:content.media:text: Aliea Brown and Mason Obradovich at their home in Robinsonville, Miss.
media:content.media:description: Aliea Brown and Mason Obradovich at their home in Robinsonville, Miss.

media:content.media:text: A cat sniffs in the corner of the home of Aliea Brown and Mason Obradovich.
media:content.media:description: Aliea Brown and Mason Obradovich at their home in Robinsonville, Miss.

item.title: Bugs and black mold: What some mobile home park residents see after investors buy in

item.dc:creator: Joe Murphy
Jiachuan Wu
Melinda Yao
item.description: Are gas prices declining? NBC News is tracking national, state and county prices at the pump.

media:content.media:text: This is a line chart showing the average U.S. gas price from Feb. 22 when it was less than $3 a gallon to April 30 when it was more than $4.20.
This is a line chart showing the average U.S. gas price from Feb. 22 when it was less than $3 a gallon to April 30 when it was more than $4.20.
This is a line chart showing the average U.S. gas price from Feb. 22 when it was less than $3 a gallon to April 30 when it was more than $4.20.

item.title: Graphic: Track U.S., state and county gas prices

item.description: After 144 years of construction, La Sagrada Familia in Barcelona is nearly complete. NBC News’ Molly Hunter was there as the pope blessed the church.

media:content.media:title: Pope Leo blesses world’s tallest church
media:content.media:description: After 144 years of construction, La Sagrada Familia in Barcelona is nearly complete. NBC News’ Molly Hunter was there as the pope blessed the church.

item.title: Pope Leo blesses world’s tallest church

item.description: Billionaire and Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates was grilled by lawmakers over his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein. Gates said that Epstein was blackmailing him over his infidelities. NBC News’ Hallie Jackson reports.

media:content.media:title: Bill Gates grilled by lawmakers over Epstein ties
media:content.media:description: Billionaire and Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates was grilled by lawmakers over his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein. Gates said that Epstein was blackmailing him over his infidelities. NBC News’ Hallie Jackson reports.

item.title: Bill Gates grilled by lawmakers over Epstein ties

item.dc:creator: Abigail Brooks
Erik Ortiz
item.description: Moments after a federal judge permanently blocked Jeffery Lee’s execution by nitrogen gas in Alabama, Lee told NBC News he was prepared to keep fighting.

media:content.media:text: Jerrery Lee is on death row at Holman Correctional Facility in Atmore, Ala.
media:content.media:description: The Holman Correctional Facility in Atmore, Ala., where the state's death chamber is located.

media:content.media:text: Jeffery Lee.
media:content.media:description: The Holman Correctional Facility in Atmore, Ala., where the state's death chamber is located.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy Office of the Alabama Attorney General
media:content.media:text: Jimmy Ellis
media:content.media:description: The Holman Correctional Facility in Atmore, Ala., where the state's death chamber is located.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy Office of the Alabama Attorney General
media:content.media:text: Elaine Thompson was shot and killed in Jimmy’s Pawn Shop in Orrville, Ala., in 1998.
media:content.media:description: The Holman Correctional Facility in Atmore, Ala., where the state's death chamber is located.

item.title: Jeffery Lee breathes ‘sigh of relief’ after Alabama’s nitrogen execution is deemed unconstitutional

item.dc:creator: Denise Chow
item.description: The lack of women sparked “reactions ranging from disappointment to outrage,” Administrator Jared Isaacman said.

media:content.media:text: The Artemis III crew, NASA astronaut Andre Douglas, ESA (European Space Agency) astronaut Luca Parmitano, and NASA astronauts Randy Bresnik and Frank Rubio on June 9, 2026.
media:content.media:description: The Artemis III crew — NASA astronaut Andre Douglas, European Space Agency astronaut Luca Parmitano and NASA astronauts Randy Bresnik and Frank Rubio — on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman Testifies During Congressional Hearing
media:content.media:description: The Artemis III crew — NASA astronaut Andre Douglas, European Space Agency astronaut Luca Parmitano and NASA astronauts Randy Bresnik and Frank Rubio — on Tuesday.

item.title: NASA addresses criticism over all-male crew selected for Artemis III test mission

item.description: From perennial powerhouses in Europe and South America to underdogs from around the globe, many of soccer’s very best are converging on the United States for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. NBC’s Jesse Kirsch reports for TODAY with the top players to watch.

media:content.media:title: Here Are the Top Players to Watch in the 2026 FIFA World Cup
media:content.media:description: From perennial powerhouses in Europe and South America to underdogs from around the globe, many of soccer’s very best are converging on the United States for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. NBC’s Jesse Kirsch reports for TODAY with the top players to watch.

item.title: Here Are the Top Players to Watch in the 2026 FIFA World Cup

item.dc:creator: Alexander Smith
Abigail Williams
item.description: Somalia isn’t competing at the World Cup, but from the scenes at Mogadishu’s airport, you’d think the country had won the trophy.

media:content.media:text: Somali international referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan waves to supporters while draped in the Somali flag as he is welcomed ahead of a solidarity football match after returning to Somalia in Mogadishu on June 10, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Somali international referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan waves to supporters while draped in the Somali flag as he is welcomed ahead of a solidarity football match after returning to Somalia in Mogadishu on June 10, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Aymen Hussein of Iraq reacts during a match against Bolivia on March 31, 2026 in Guadalupe, Mexico.
media:content.media:description: Aymen Hussein of Iraq reacts against Bolivia in Guadalupe, Mexico, on March 31.

item.title: Referee barred from entering U.S. for World Cup gets a hero’s welcome in Somalia

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
Rebecca Cohen
item.description: Unite Here Local 11, which represents nearly 2,000 cooks, dishwashers, bartenders and concession workers at SoFi Stadium, voted just last week to authorize a strike.

media:content.media:text: SoFi Stadium workers arrive at union headquarters
media:content.media:description: SoFi Stadium workers arrive at union headquarters in Inglewood, Calif., for a strike vote Thursday.

item.title: Workers at L.A.-area World Cup stadium reach tentative agreement after authorizing strike

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: Raquel Coronell Uribe
item.description: The president suggested the recent numbers mean there will be a bigger decline in inflation once the war in Iran is over.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Signs The Secure America Act
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump during a signing ceremony for the "Secure America Act" in the Oval Office of the White House on June 10, 2026.

item.title: Trump says ‘I love the inflation’ as annual rate jumps to a 3-year high

item.dc:creator: Tim Stelloh
Bill Feather
Andrew Blankstein
item.description: A San Diego man previously accused in a 2023 murder was charged Wednesday with killing three more people, officials said

media:content.media:text: The FBI, CHP and San Diego police investigated a home in Southcrest after human remains were found on Tuesday.
media:content.media:description: The FBI, the California Highway Patrol and San Diego police investigated a home in San Diego's Southcrest neighborhood after human remains were found Tuesday.

item.title: Slaying suspect in San Diego charged with 3 more murders, including 2 cold cases

item.dc:creator: Corky Siemaszko
item.description: An Ohio man and self-described “incel” who was convicted of plotting a sorority mass shooting now lives two blocks from one of his potential targets — the Ohio State University campus

media:content.media:text: Tres Genco, a 22-year-old from Hillsboro, Ohio, charged with hate crime related to a plot to conduct mass shooting of women.
media:content.media:description: Tres Genco, a 22-year-old from Hillsboro, Ohio, charged with hate crime related to a plot to conduct mass shooting of women.

item.title: Man convicted in plot to shoot up Ohio State sorority now lives two blocks from campus

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: In today’s edition, Ben Kamisar takes stock of the half-billion dollars Tom Steyer has spent over the course of two unsuccessful bids for office.

media:content.media:text: Tom Steyer.
media:content.media:description: Tom Steyer in San Francisco on May 26, 2026.

item.title: The limits of self-funding: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Alicia Victoria Lozano
Madeline Morrison
item.description: A New Year’s Day fire led to the destructive Palisades Fire. Jonathan Rinderknecht is charged with three federal crimes.

media:content.media:text: A helicopter drops water as the Palisades Fire grows
media:content.media:description: A helicopter drops water as the Palisades Fire grows in the Pacific Palisades neighborhood of Los Angeles on Jan. 7, 2025.

media:content.media:credit: U.S. Attorney's Office for the Central District of California
media:content.media:text: Arson suspect indicted on 2 more felony counts in deadly Los Angeles Palisades fire
media:content.media:description: A helicopter drops water as the Palisades Fire grows in the Pacific Palisades neighborhood of Los Angeles on Jan. 7, 2025.

item.title: Prosecutors accuse man of ‘maliciously’ starting Palisades Fire after failed relationship

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
item.description: An Indiana woman who was said to have been missing in Mexico for months with her seven children was found dead, her family said.

media:content.media:credit: via The National Center for Missing & Exploited Children
media:content.media:text: Makala Pendley.
media:content.media:description: Makala Pendley.

media:content.media:credit: via The National Center for Missing & Exploited Children
media:content.media:text: Makala Pendley.
media:content.media:description: Makala Pendley.

item.title: Indiana mother of 7 who went missing in Mexico is found dead, her family says

item.dc:creator: NBC Washington
item.description: Kiany DeJesus was just 11 years old when her mother was killed by her ex-boyfriend in Greenbelt, Maryland

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of family via NBC Washington
media:content.media:text: Emilia Ignacio, left, and her daughter Kiany DeJesus pose for a portrait outside in front of trees
media:content.media:description: Emilia Ignacio, left, and her daughter Kiany DeJesus.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of family via NBC Washington
media:content.media:text: Emilia Ignacio
media:content.media:description: Emilia Ignacio, left, and her daughter Kiany DeJesus.

media:content.media:text: Kiany DeJesus speaks while standing outside in front of a building
media:content.media:description: Emilia Ignacio, left, and her daughter Kiany DeJesus.

item.title: Woman used social media to catch man who killed her mom

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
Rohan Nadkarni
item.description: Forget trying to predict the Stanley Cup Final.

media:content.media:text: Jordan Staal of the Carolina Hurricanes scores a goal against Colton Sissons of the Vegas Golden Knights in the third period of game four of the Stanley Cup Finals on June 9, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Jordan Staal of the Carolina Hurricanes scores Tuesday against Colton Sissons of the Vegas Golden Knights in Game 4 of the Stanley Cup Final.

media:content.media:text: Brandon Bussi of the Carolina Hurricanes makes a save against the Vegas Golden Knights
media:content.media:description: Jordan Staal of the Carolina Hurricanes scores Tuesday against Colton Sissons of the Vegas Golden Knights in Game 4 of the Stanley Cup Final.

media:content.media:text: Valverde Federico of Uruguay controls the ball during a friendly match against Algeria on March 31, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Jordan Staal of the Carolina Hurricanes scores Tuesday against Colton Sissons of the Vegas Golden Knights in Game 4 of the Stanley Cup Final.

item.title: From The Sports Desk: Carolina claims Game 4 in back-and-forth Stanley Cup Final

item.dc:creator: Lindsay Good
David K. Li
item.description: A mother and her two children struggled in the water before witnesses jumped in and rescued two of the three, Laguna Beach Marine Safety said.

media:content.media:text: An Orange County Sheriff’s Department patrol boat on June 10, 2026.
media:content.media:description: An Orange County Sheriff’s Department patrol boat on June 10, 2026.

item.title: Girl swept away and still missing off Southern California

item.dc:creator: Gordon Lubold
Courtney Kube
Carol E. Lee
item.description: The president is expected to press the leaders to find ways to swiftly increase production of weaponry for the Pentagon, sources told NBC News.

media:content.media:text: Trump Signs Order Directing Customs Crackdown Amid Trade Gaps
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump during an executive order signing in the Oval Office of the White House in Washington, DC, US, on Wednesday, June 3, 2026. Trump signed an executive order designed to tighten customs enforcement, his latest move to ramp up protectionist trade policies.

media:content.media:text: An image U.S. Central Command shared on X alongside a post about the U.S.' completing strikes in response to Iran’s attack on an Apache helicopter.
media:content.media:description: An image U.S. Central Command shared on X alongside a post about the U.S.' completing strikes in response to Iran’s attack on an Apache helicopter.

item.title: Defense industry leaders prepare to meet with Trump amid missile supply worries, sources say

item.dc:creator: NBC News
item.description: Stephanie Gosk will answer your questions about the 2026 World Cup live on Thursday, June 11, at 12 p.m. ET from Mexico City ahead of Mexico vs. South Africa.

media:content.media:text: TODAY -- Pictured: Stephanie Gosk on Monday, June 10, 2024
media:content.media:description: Stephanie Gosk.

media:content.media:text: South Africa vs Mexico.
media:content.media:description: Stephanie Gosk.

item.title: Stephanie Gosk answers your questions about the World Cup and more
